steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for one difference between the words “cocoa” and “alpaca” according to the text. The correct answer must be directly supported by what the passage states — not inferred, extended, or introduced from outside the text.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — “cocoa” entered English recently, “alpaca” entered English many centuries ago: the passage says nothing about when either word entered English. ✗
  • Choice B — “cocoa” traces to Nahuatl, “alpaca” traces to Quechua: the passage states explicitly that Spanish borrowed “cocoa” from Nahuatl and that “alpaca” comes from Quechua. This is the one difference the text directly identifies. ✓
  • Choice C — “cocoa” changed in meaning, “alpaca” retained its meaning: the passage makes no claim about meaning changes during borrowing. ✗
  • Choice D — “cocoa” is known as Indigenous, “alpaca” is mistakenly seen as Spanish: the passage says nothing about public misconceptions regarding either word’s origin. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
